neighborhoods.name: Gornji Grad (Upper Town)
neighborhoods.description: Medieval hilltop core with St. Mark Church, government buildings, and cobblestone streets
neighborhoods.character: Historic hilltop, government quarter, cultural hub
neighborhoods.character_notes: The historic and commercial heart of Zagreb. Most walkable area with best access to cultural attractions, restaurants, and public services.

neighborhoods.name: Maksimir
neighborhoods.description: Eastern residential area near the large Maksimir Park and zoo with green spaces and family homes

housing.predominantType: Houses, older apartments, and newer developments

neighborhoods.character_notes: Established residential area offering a quieter lifestyle while maintaining reasonable access to Zagreb's center and services.

neighborhoods.name: Novi Zagreb (New Zagreb)
neighborhoods.description: Southern district across the Sava river with socialist-era housing and modern shopping centers

housing.predominantType: Socialist-era apartment blocks and new-build condos

neighborhoods.character_notes: More affordable option for budget-conscious retirees. May require a car or longer transit times to reach central amenities.

neighborhoods.name: Donji Grad (Lower Town)
neighborhoods.description: Grand 19th-century planned district with green squares, Art Nouveau buildings, and the main train station
neighborhoods.character: Elegant urban, cultural institutions, cafe culture

housing.predominantType: Apartments in Austro-Hungarian and Art Nouveau buildings

neighborhoods.character_notes: Area with established international community and expat-oriented services. English is more commonly spoken here than in other neighborhoods.
